records.ety: [Freq. of wade; cf. AS. wædlian to beg, from wadan to go. See Wade.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Waddled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Waddling (?).]

defs: To walk with short steps, swaying the body from one side to the other, like a duck or very fat person; to move clumsily and totteringly along; to toddle; to stumble; as, a child waddles when he begins to walk; a goose waddles.
quotes: She drawls her words, and waddles in her pace.

defs: To trample or tread down, as high grass, by walking through it.
